text: From our experience with Homogeneous Reactor Experiment-2 (HRE-2 or HRT) and the Molten-Salt Reactor Experiment (MSRE), the answer is an unqualified yes.
A reference conceptual design for a 1000-Mw(e) molten-salt thermal breeder reactor (MSBR) is described in ORNL-3996.
The program for developing the breeder through a molten-salt breeder experiment (MSBE) is summarized in ORNL-TM-1851.
This portable shield provides 12 in. of steel for shielding (attenuation factor $10^{4}-10^{5}$ ), tool access holes, lighting, and maneuverability.
The disconnects for the 5-in. sched-40 piping are called freeze flanges.
The drain and storage system of the reactor is connected with 1-1/2-in. sched-40 piping.
The equipment to accomplish this is on hand<sup>3</sup> and has been extensively tested in mockups, but not yet in a radioactive situation.
For example, the reactor vessel for a 250 Mw(e) MSBR module weighs 71 tons compared to 9 tons for the MSRE.
The power level in an MSBR module is higher by a factor of about 80 and the residual activity after the fuel salt is drained would be correspondingly higher.

The pipeline industry has automatic equipment that will make high quality welds on 30-in.-diam pipe.

When the reactor is operating at $7\mathrm{Mw}$ , the level is $60,000\mathrm{r/hr}$ .
This drops to $4000\mathrm{r/hr}$ immediately after draining the fuel.

During a recent shutdown the radiation level in the cell was $2000\mathrm{r/hr}$ seven days after shutdown while maintenance operations were in progress and the work was accomplished without undue exposure of personnel.
